quoteTime: about six o'clock in the evening
quoteLast: of Tuesday, August 21, that Captain Bill and his little force of two reached Brownsville. The Captain immediately paid a visit to Mayor Combe, and to Chairman of the Citizens' Committee Kelley.
title: Captain Bill McDonald, Texas Ranger: A Story of Frontier Reform

title: The Queen's cadet, and other tales

quoteTime: about six o'clock in the evening
quoteLast: and the men had not returned from work, but Carrie was cooking and got up with a cry of welcome when he came out of the woods.  She stopped, however, when she saw his gloomy face.
title: Partners of the Out-Trail
author: Bindloss, Harold, 1866-1945

quoteFirst: For four or five miles before we approached the town, the laughing and animated faces of groups of peasantry, all in their jubilee dresses, the old mounted upon asses, and the young walking by the sides of them, hastening to the town, announced to us, that a fair, and merry making was to be held there, on that day. Lisieux was quite in a bustle.
quoteTime: About six o'clock in the evening
quoteLast: of the same day, we arrived at Caen, the capital of Lower Normandy. My fare to this city from Paris, amounted to thirty livres, including my luggage.
title: The stranger in France
author: Carr, John, Sir, 1772-1832

quoteFirst: I myself was carried thither, having received five wounds in different parts of the body, my left hand shattered, and two nasty wounds in the head. I was totally unconscious when taken out of the Redan, and for some hours afterwards. At
quoteLast: , I found myself in our front trench, with a dead 33rd man lying across me; I got him off the best way I could, and then tried to get up, but found that I could not stand, for I had almost bled to death. Dr. Hale, V.C., did all he could for me; I then had to remain and take my chance or turn of being carried to camp, where I arrived about 7·30 p.m., when my wounds were dressed, and a good cup of beef tea revived me; there I had to remain for upwards of three months, but, with careful attendance, and a good, strong constitution, I was, by that time, ready for them
title:
A Soldier's Experience; or, A Voice from the Ranks
Showing the Cost of War in Blood and Treasure. A Personal Narrative of the Crimean Campaign, from the Standpoint of the Ranks; the Indian Mutiny, and Some of its Atrocities; the Afghan Campaigns of 1863
author: Gowing, T. (Timothy), 1834-1908

quoteTime: About six o'clock in the evening

title: The Bravest of the Brave — or, with Peterborough in Spain
author: Henty, G. A. (George Alfred), 1832-1902

quoteTime: about six o'clock in the evening
quoteLast: when the sea-breeze is making its final efforts, I have perceived it to blow with a considerable degree of warmth, owing to the heat the sea had by that time acquired, which would soon begin to divert the current of air towards it when it had first overcome the vis inertiae that preserves motion in a body after the impelling power has ceased to operate.
title: The history of Sumatra
author: Marsden, William, 1754-1836
